what_it_does: Defines a shared 1600×900 visual scaffold for presentation 'pieces': a centered fixed canvas with warm radial page background, masked blueprint corner grids, a title stage, a phone/content stage, a goldwash hatched footer, and a circular mark, plus a generated :root block of CSS custom properties as a standalone token fallback.
observations: Fixed 1600×900 .view canvas centered in the viewport.
Two blueprint-grid corners (.bp.tr and .bp.tl) with radial masks and very low opacity.
.phones area is an absolutely positioned flex container for piece content.
Footer uses a gradient wash plus a repeating -45° ochre hatch masked from bottom.
Large generated :root token section bracketed by DNA:VARS START/END comments.
Tokens cover surfaces, identity palette, spacing, radii, typography, shadows, layout margin, and device dimensions.
Comment states the block is generated by engine/build/surface.mjs from dna/tokens.json + dna/grammar.json and is overridden live by DNA.injectVars.
standouts: The generated DNA variable block acts as a standalone fallback for a live-injected token system.
Fixed 1600×900 artboard metaphor with decorative blueprint corners.
Goldwash hatched footer and circular mark as consistent chrome.
Color values are entirely routed through CSS custom properties.
conventions: Minimal CSS reset at the top.
Single-class selectors rather than deep nesting.
CSS custom properties used for all colors, spacing, type, and radii.
Generated token region delimited by explicit START/END markers.
Both -webkit- and standard mask-image declarations for compatibility.
Comments used as inline documentation and provenance notes.
concerns: Hardcoded 1600×900 canvas is not responsive to smaller viewports.
.phones absolute top/bottom bounds may clip content that exceeds the stage.
Blueprint grid opacity is extremely low (0.055), which may be invisible on some displays.
The generated :root block is large and could become stale if tokens change and regeneration is missed.
No @media queries or dark-mode tokens are present.
External font 'Plus Jakarta Sans' is used but not loaded within this file.
notes: This is the shared CSS shell for a fixed 1600×900 'piece' presentation, carrying generated DNA tokens and decorative blueprint/goldwash chrome.
questions: How and where is DNA.injectVars implemented?
What markup is expected inside the .phones stage?
Is this file consumed directly, or bundled/preprocessed elsewhere?
Are the --dna-device-* dimensions used by other files, or are they dead tokens here?
Is there a separate non-fallback token file that should be considered canonical?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it provides a reusable visual scaffold and a generated token fallback for individual pieces.
apparent_themes: Warm paper, blueprint, and gold identity
Generated design tokens as source of truth
Live themeability through CSS custom properties
intention_signals: To give every design piece a consistent, brand-consistent presentation stage while allowing colors and tokens to be overridden live via an injected DNA token system.
novelty: A fixed 1600×900 'piece' canvas combined with a generated, injectable DNA token fallback and decorative blueprint-corner/goldwash-hatch chrome.
contribution: Provides a shared baseline styling contract for 1600×900 pieces, including the layout shell, decorative motifs, and a generated CSS custom-property vocabulary that a fused system can keep as the fallback token surface.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS scaffold file for a fixed 1600×900 design piece with radial page background, masked blueprint corner grids, title/phone-stage/footer/mark chrome, and a generated :root DNA token fallback for colors, spacing, typography, shadows, and device dimensions.
intention_for_embedding: To give every piece a consistent, live-themeable presentation shell without redeclaring layout or tokens.
